pipeline_status.poller: QUIET — 9+ consecutive quiet polls. Dome site unchanged (V51.0, 67 WINs, same parameters). Latest commit d6c8db9 (2026-04-06). Internal inconsistencies stable: homepage 95.2% vs wins page 98.5%, evolution page still references V50.6.
pipeline_status.analyst: EXP-016 (Section 4.5.1 H(r) reconciliation) completed. All expansion queue items now done. EXP-015 and EXP-016 awaiting decider integration patches (written this run). Next run: Mode 3 (Globe Fingerprint Hunt).
pipeline_status.curmudgeon: Cycle 2 Phase 1: 17/101 items reviewed (WIN-001 through WIN-017). Last review: WIN-017 (Cycle 2, confidence 0.97). Current: parallex as firmament wobble — verdict confirmed Refuted by Data. New Horizons evidence identified as moderate gap.

external_changes.dome_site_changes: No changes detected across 9 consecutive polls. All parameters stable. Two internal inconsistencies noted: homepage shows 95.2% (67/71 denominator), wins page shows 98.5% (66/67 denominator) — different denominators for same metric.

expansions_integrated_this_run.summary: Full Meyl near-field technical expansion: apparatus description, Bruhn/Kuhlke mathematical refutation, theory-vs-experiment distinction, CUORE/LIGO/ADMX global evidence, dome code analysis. 446→3,765 chars.

H(r) reconciliation impossibility: supremum of H(r) is 8,537 km; two of three target heights (9,572 and 9,086 km) exceed this — mathematically impossible. Post-hoc radius selection critique added. 1,294→3,987 chars.

holes_found.description: New Horizons interstellar parallax (April 2020) not mentioned. Patch written.
'both methods agree' should be 'all methods agree'. Patch written.

internal_issues.description: New Horizons interstellar parallax (April 2020) not in detail_evidence. From 7 billion km, measured Proxima Cen (~32.4") and Wolf 359 (~15.7") parallax — impossible under any dome model.

suggested_patch.find: both methods agree, ruling out any global systematic that would affect geometric parallax alone.
suggested_patch.replace: all methods agree, ruling out any global systematic that would affect geometric parallax alone. Spacecraft-based measurements provide even stronger evidence: NASA's New Horizons probe, located 7 billion km from Earth in April 2020, measured parallax shifts of 32.4 arcsec (Proxima Centauri) and 15.7 arcsec (Wolf 359) that scale precisely with each star's distance. Under the dome model, New Horizons would be ~770,000× further than the firmament height; no dome-embedded star could be observed, let alone measured with distance-dependent parallax.

internal_issues.description: 'both methods agree' — three methods are listed, making 'both' incorrect.
